locked_facts: Vector addition is commutative: A + B = B + A
Triangle law: place vectors head to tail — resultant goes from tail of first to head of second
Parallelogram law: |R| = sqrt(A² + B² + 2AB·cosθ) where θ is angle between A and B
Resultant angle: tan(alpha) = B·sinθ / (A + B·cosθ) measured from vector A
Components: Rx = Ax + Bx, Ry = Ay + By (add components separately)
truth_statements: Vectors are added geometrically, not algebraically — direction matters
The resultant of two vectors is found using the triangle law or parallelogram law
A vector can be resolved into two perpendicular components along x and y axes
The magnitude of resultant is maximum (A+B) when vectors are parallel and minimum (|A-B|) when antiparallel
Component method: resolve all vectors into x and y components, add components separately, then find magnitude and direction of net vector

aha_moment.what_to_show: Two vectors A and B from origin, resultant R = A+B shown as diagonal of parallelogram with numerical magnitudes — A=3, B=4, θ=90°, R=5 (not 7)
aha_moment.visual_emphasis: The resultant is 5 units, not 7 — adding magnitudes without considering direction gives the WRONG answer
aha_moment.why_this_breaks_misconception: Students believe vector magnitudes add like numbers; seeing |R|=5 instead of 7 for perpendicular 3-4 vectors shatters this belief

simulation_states.label: Daily Life
simulation_states.description: A person walks 3 km East then 4 km North. Show two displacement arrows step by step. Final position marker is 5 km from start, not 7 km. Label both arrows and show the direct path home.
simulation_states.emphasis: Walking 3 km + 4 km does NOT put you 7 km from home — direction changes everything

simulation_states.label: Triangle Law
simulation_states.description: Triangle law: place vector B at the head of vector A. Draw both arrows head to tail. The resultant R goes from the tail of A to the head of B, closing the triangle. Label all three sides.
simulation_states.emphasis: Triangle law: place vectors tip to tail — resultant closes the triangle

simulation_states.label: Formula
simulation_states.description: Parallelogram law: both vectors from the same origin. Resultant = diagonal of parallelogram. Show formula |R| = sqrt(A²+B²+2ABcosθ) with numbers: A=3, B=4, θ=90° → R = sqrt(9+16) = 5. Highlight that 3+4=7 is WRONG.
simulation_states.emphasis: Parallelogram diagonal is the resultant. R = sqrt(A²+B²) for perpendicular vectors

simulation_states.label: Explore
simulation_states.description: Interactive: student changes magnitudes of A and B and angle θ. Resultant arrow and formula value update in real time. Show that at θ=0° resultant is maximum (A+B), at θ=180° resultant is minimum (|A-B|).
simulation_states.emphasis: Try θ=0° (maximum) and θ=180° (minimum) to see the range of possible resultants

common_misconceptions.belief: Vector magnitudes add like numbers — two 3N forces give 6N resultant regardless of direction
common_misconceptions.correction: NCERT says vectors add geometrically — resultant depends on angle. Two 3N forces at 90° give only 4.24N resultant (sqrt(18)), not 6N
common_misconceptions.simulation_emphasis: Show two 3-unit vectors at 90° producing a 4.24-unit resultant, not 6 units

typical_question_types: resultant of two forces at angle θ
common_traps: confusing angle between vectors with angle resultant makes with one vector
forgetting resultant of equal vectors at 120° is zero
